channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: On July 7, a small Miami company called City Labs put a tritium-powered battery the size of a pencil eraser into orbit aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 — its softball-sized BOHR CubeSat becoming the first commercial spacecraft ever cleared to carry a nuclear power - Space Daily
item.description:
1. On July 7, a small Miami company called City Labs put a tritium-powered battery the size of a pencil eraser into orbit aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 — its softball-sized BOHR CubeSat becoming the first commercial spacecraft ever cleared to carry a nuclear power  Space Daily
  2. Transporter-17 Mission  SpaceX
  3. Miami-based City Labs achieves a first for commercial nuclear power in space  Ars Technica
  4. SpaceX launches Transporter-17 amid concerns about rideshare program’s future  SpaceNews

item.title: What happens to your brain in space? - BBC
item.description:
1. What happens to your brain in space?  BBC
  2. Brain scans of ISS astronauts show the organ quietly re-weighting its own senses during six months in orbit — turning down the silent inner ear and leaning almost entirely on the eyes to build a world with no down  Space Daily
  3. What happens to our brain in space? The surprising truth discovered by scientists  Zamin.uz
  4. Repeat Space Missions Do Not Appear to Worsen Eye or Brain Structural Changes: JAMA  Medical Dialogues

item.title: In November 2025, UC Irvine astronomers announced a rocky super-Earth called GJ 251 c orbiting in a habitable zone less than 20 light-years away — close enough that the next generation of ground-based telescopes may actually photograph it directly - Space Daily
item.description:
1. In November 2025, UC Irvine astronomers announced a rocky super-Earth called GJ 251 c orbiting in a habitable zone less than 20 light-years away — close enough that the next generation of ground-based telescopes may actually photograph it directly  Space Daily
  2. This Nearby Exoplanet Is Likely Nice And Rocky. But It Also Sits On The Dangerous "Cosmic Shoreline"  IFLScience
  3. Astronomers discover a potentially habitable planet just 25 light-years away. 'This one is exciting'  Space
  4. Alien life on nearby 'super Earth' much likelier than we thought, study claims  Live Science
  5. UC Irvine astronomers discover a new Earth-like exoplanet  UC Irvine News

item.title: In a 3.5-billion-year-old clay-rich rock from Gale Crater, NASA’s Curiosity rover identified 21 carbon-based molecules — including a nitrogen-bearing ring structure linked to the chemistry that can precede RNA and DNA — showing that ancient Martian sedime - Space Daily
item.description:
1. In a 3.5-billion-year-old clay-rich rock from Gale Crater, NASA’s Curiosity rover identified 21 carbon-based molecules — including a nitrogen-bearing ring structure linked to the chemistry that can precede RNA and DNA — showing that ancient Martian sedime  Space Daily
  2. Could evidence of life on Mars be hiding in clay? Europe wants to send a rover to check  Space
  3. A martian rock has lots of carbon on it, and it’s not clear why  Ars Technica
  4. NASA rover takes a closer look at organic carbon on Mars  Reuters
  5. A Surprising Meteorite Discovery Could Change the Hunt for Life on Mars  SciTechDaily

item.title: Voyager 1 will reach a new milestone in November 2026: it will be so far from Earth that a radio signal travelling at the speed of light will take a full 24 hours to reach it — a distance humanity’s most distant spacecraft crossed in just over 49 years of continuous fl - Space Daily
item.description:
1. Voyager 1 will reach a new milestone in November 2026: it will be so far from Earth that a radio signal travelling at the speed of light will take a full 24 hours to reach it — a distance humanity’s most distant spacecraft crossed in just over 49 years of continuous fl  Space Daily
  2. The Voyager 1 probe, launched in 1977, is now so far from Earth that a radio signal travelling at the speed of light takes over 22 hours to reach it, and it is still sending data back from interstellar space on a power source the size of a car battery  Space Daily
  3. 50 Years Later, NASA's Voyager Probe Is Reaching A Big Milestone For Space Travel  bgr.com
  4. NASA’s Voyager 1 left Earth in 1977; now its signal takes over 22 hours to reach us and it is still sending data  The Times of India
  5. The spacecraft that refused to quit: How Voyager 1 keeps talking to Earth after 48 years  Business Today

item.title: In nearly seventy years of deep-sea exploration, researchers compiled 43,681 submersible dive records — and found that humans have visually observed less than 0.001% of the deep seafloor, an area roughly the size of Rhode Island, according to a Science - Space Daily
item.description:
1. In nearly seventy years of deep-sea exploration, researchers compiled 43,681 submersible dive records — and found that humans have visually observed less than 0.001% of the deep seafloor, an area roughly the size of Rhode Island, according to a Science  Space Daily
  2. The Vessel That Never Sleeps  Geo Week News
  3. Less than 30% of Earth’s ocean floor has been mapped while scientists still have clearer high-resolution data of Mars than most of the seabed covering our own planet  The Times of India
  4. Here’s Why: The ocean is way deeper than you think  WZZM13.com
  5. We have mapped the surface of Mars, the Moon and Venus in sharper detail than the floor of our own ocean, most of which no human instrument has ever seen up close  Space Daily

  5. 450-Million-Year-Old Sea Lily Fossil Preserves Soft Tissue Against the Odds  GreekReporter.com

  5. Rejected by NASA at 14, she took a work placement cleaning a space toilet instead; today, she is leading Mars exploration teams  The Times of India

item.title: A neutron star can pack more mass than our entire Sun into a sphere only about 20 kilometres across — roughly the size of a city — and a teaspoon of its material would weigh about a billion tonnes on Earth, more than every human being alive combined. - Space Daily

item.title: Proxima Centauri b is our closest known habitable-zone exoplanet, just over four light-years away, and it may be warm enough for liquid water if it has kept an atmosphere. But that “if” is enormous: its red-dwarf star blasts it with extreme radiation strong enough - Space Daily
item.description: Proxima Centauri b is our closest known habitable-zone exoplanet, just over four light-years away, and it may be warm enough for liquid water if it has kept an atmosphere. But that “if” is enormous: its red-dwarf star blasts it with extreme radiation strong enough  Space Daily

item.description:

  3. Mysterious Compound Detected on Pluto and Titan  WIRED
  4. The James Webb Space Telescope has detected the same unexplained absorption signal on the surfaces of Pluto and Titan — two frozen worlds with very different environments but methane-and-nitrogen chemistry in common — and so far, scientists cannot  Space Daily
  5. Both Pluto and Titan Share a Mystery Molecule That’s Never Been Seen Before  Gizmodo

item.title: Neutrinos Are Worth a Thousand Words! - Avi Loeb – Medium
item.description:
